title: Pomazota bracteata
intro: Pomazota bracteata, also known as Mexican prickly poppy or popote, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the family Papaveraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and analgesic effects, making it useful in treating conditions such as pain, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pomazota bracteata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects.
taxonomy: Pomazota bracteata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is a member of the genus Pomazota, which is a group of plants that are native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The species is further classified as a perennial herb, with a growth habit that is characteristic of its family. The classification of Pomazota bracteata is based on its morphology, anatomy, and genetic characteristics. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification and relationships within the genus Pomazota.

properties: Pomazota bracteata has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and stems have been used in herbal remedies to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
